jobTitle: Vibe / Claude Coding Tutor — Art Print Storefront
primaryAsk: Tutor who teaches a non-developer how to build and maintain a custom Shopify theme using Claude-assisted development (vibe coding). The deeper value is not code delivery but teaching the client to prompt Claude well, review outputs critically, and build intuition for AI-assisted Shopify development. Screen-share walkthroughs, explain the why behind decisions, and help craft iterative Claude prompts.

clientContext.who: Fine-art photographer building a custom Shopify storefront to sell photography prints.
clientContext.vision: Minimal, editorial, gallery-forward design inspired by photographer portfolio sites.
clientContext.coreNeed: Hands-on guidance to translate vision into a real, functioning Shopify theme (Liquid, JavaScript, Shopify native tools) and learn how to maintain/extend it using Claude as a core part of the workflow.
clientContext.learningGoal: Client wants to own the codebase and be able to modify and extend the theme themselves going forward, with Claude as a day-to-day partner.

sessionStyle.format: Real-time screen-share walkthroughs where you build together
sessionStyle.teaching: Explain the why behind each decision, not just the what
sessionStyle.promptCrafting: Help client craft and iterate on Claude prompts that produce quality Shopify code
sessionStyle.documentation: Occasional short written summaries or checklists after each session

pages: Homepage — horizontal scroll gallery with drag and arrow navigation
Product page — editorial image view, print size selector, edition info, add to cart
Collection page — grid or index-style listing of available prints
keyFeatures: Custom Liquid sections for the horizontal gallery
Variant-based product options for print sizes and editions
Metafields for edition numbering and print details
idealCandidateSignals: Comfortable working alongside AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, etc.)
Experience tutoring or teaching non-developers — patience and clarity matter
Ability to explain code decisions in plain language
Portfolio or examples of Shopify theme work

michaelNotes.backendRequirement: Real mw-backend integration with JWT-based mock login and mock checkout flow
michaelNotes.imageSource: Free-to-use images (Unsplash, Pexels, Picsum, or public-domain art)

proposalFraming.emphasize: Ability to communicate well and coach the client on Claude-assisted development
proposalFraming.talked_to_daily: Daily experience in full-time job and freelancing building websites using Claude, Codex, and GitHub Copilot
proposalFraming.positioning: You are not just a Shopify expert; you are a tutor who uses Claude as a daily partner and can teach others to do the same

demoDirection.concept: Working, gallery-forward art-print storefront demo that serves as a teaching artifact

hero.feature: Horizontal scrolling gallery with drag + arrow navigation
hero.flow: Homepage gallery → Product page with size/edition variant selector and live price update → Add to cart → Mock cart → Mock checkout

supporting: Mock JWT login (mirror existing auth pattern in mw-backend)
Mock checkout endpoint returning a confirmed order
demoDirection.architecture: Static same-origin app (not a Shopify dev store) with real mw-backend blueprint, but framed as the exact OS 2.0 'sections and blocks' architecture the client will rebuild in Liquid
demoDirection.teachingArtifact: Embedded 'How I'd coach this with Claude' panel showing: the prompt you'd write, what to review, what errors to catch — turns the demo into proof of tutoring value

endpoints: Mock login (JWT, no real auth core changes)

scopeConstraints.demoScope: 1 hero feature (gallery + variant-to-cart) + at most 2 supporting (login, checkout)
scopeConstraints.pages: Not all 5 pages required in demo; show the hero flow well and the tutoring angle, not every page
scopeConstraints.performance: Lean full: vanilla JS / CDN-light build. Model the 'fast load, minimal deps' principle in the demo itself
scopeConstraints.notRequired: Do not set up an actual Shopify dev store; this is a tutor's portfolio proof, not production

gatingConstraints: Applicant must be US-based (explicit gate in posting)
demoFidelity: Full, functional prototype (not mockup-only). Clicks through a real hero flow with seeded mock data.
